_meta.dna: web(universal) = responsive(mobile_first) x pwa(offline) x seo(discoverable) x cloud_twin(persistent) x api(documented) x cdn(fast) x a11y(wcag_aaa)
_meta.question_expansion_instruction: Each floor lists 7 seed questions. The web is the universal platform — no install, instant access, searchable, shareable. The cloud twin runs Solace Browser in the cloud. Every question must probe whether the web experience is compelling enough to convert visitors to desktop/mobile users.

name: Tower of Web
protocol: FORECAST -> CROSS-REF -> AUDIT -> FIX -> VERIFY -> SEAL -> POSTMORTEM

questions: Does every page work on viewports from 320px to 2560px?
Are breakpoints consistent across all pages (mobile, tablet, desktop)?
Does the navigation collapse to a hamburger menu on mobile?
Are images responsive (srcset, picture element) for different viewports?
Do touch targets meet minimum size (44px) on mobile viewports?
Does the home page layout adapt gracefully from phone to ultrawide?
Are CSS Grid and Flexbox used for robust responsive layouts?
Does the web app have a complete manifest.json (name, icons, theme_color)?
Is there a service worker for offline capability?
Does the PWA install prompt appear on supported browsers?
Can the PWA work offline (cached pages, offline indicator)?
Does the PWA support push notifications?
Are app icons correct for all sizes (192x192, 512x512, maskable)?
Does the PWA pass the Lighthouse PWA audit?
Does every page have unique title and meta description?
Is there structured data (JSON-LD) for rich search results?
Does the site have a sitemap.xml with all public pages?
Is there a robots.txt that allows search engine crawling?
Are canonical URLs set correctly (no duplicate content)?
Does the site use semantic HTML (h1-h6, article, nav, main)?
Are Open Graph and Twitter Card meta tags on all pages?
Can the cloud twin provide a full browser experience in the browser?
Is the cloud twin deployed on scalable infrastructure (Cloud Run)?
Can the cloud twin persist sessions across reconnections?
Is the latency acceptable for interactive use (<200ms round-trip)?
Can the cloud twin handle multiple concurrent users?
Does the cloud twin use the same theme and settings as local?
Is the cloud twin cost-effective for the pricing model?
Does the landing page clearly communicate what Solace Browser is?
Is there a clear call-to-action (download, try free) above the fold?
Does the page load in under 2 seconds (LCP)?
Are social proof elements present (testimonials, user count)?
Does the pricing page clearly explain tiers and value?
Is the download page easy to navigate (auto-detect OS)?
Does the blog provide valuable content for SEO?
Does the web app work in Chromium, Firefox, Safari, and Edge?
Are there browser-specific CSS issues (webkit, moz prefixes)?
Does the app handle different JavaScript engine behaviors?
Are there polyfills for features not supported in older browsers?
Does the app work in iOS Safari (WebKit) correctly?
Are there Chromium vs Firefox rendering differences?
Does the app handle browser extensions that modify pages?
Is there an OpenAPI/Swagger specification for all public APIs?
Does the API documentation include request/response examples?
Is there an interactive API explorer (try-it-now)?
Are authentication requirements documented per endpoint?
Are rate limits and quota documented?
Is there a changelog for API version changes?
Are error response formats consistent and documented?
Are static assets served from a CDN?
Are cache headers (Cache-Control, ETag) set correctly?
Is there cache busting for updated assets (hash in filename)?
Are CDN costs optimized (not serving uncacheable content)?
Does the CDN support HTTP/2 or HTTP/3?
Is there edge computing for API responses?
Does the CDN cover the target geographic regions?
Is HTTPS enforced on all pages (HTTP redirects to HTTPS)?
Is HSTS configured with includeSubDomains and preload?
Are TLS 1.2 and 1.3 supported (not older versions)?
Is the certificate chain complete and valid?
Does the site have an A+ rating on SSL Labs?
Are mixed content warnings eliminated?
Is OCSP stapling enabled for faster TLS handshakes?
Does the website pass WCAG 2.1 AA for all public pages?
Are all images alt-tagged appropriately?
Is keyboard navigation working on all pages?
Are form labels associated with inputs correctly?
Do error messages identify the problem and suggest solutions?
Are ARIA landmarks used correctly (main, nav, banner)?
Does the color contrast meet AA ratios (4.5:1 text, 3:1 large)?
What is the Lighthouse performance score for the home page?
Are images in next-gen formats (WebP, AVIF)?
Is JavaScript minified and code-split?
Is CSS minified and critical CSS inlined?
Are third-party scripts deferred or async?
Is there a performance budget enforced in CI?
Does the site achieve green Core Web Vitals?
Is there privacy-respecting analytics (no GA4, use Plausible/Fathom)?
Are conversion events tracked (download, signup, purchase)?
Is there funnel analysis for the onboarding flow?
Can the analytics run without cookies (cookie-free)?
Are analytics compliant with GDPR (no consent required if cookie-free)?
Is there A/B testing infrastructure for landing page optimization?
Are analytics data accessible to the team in real-time?
Can the web app send transactional emails (verification, password reset)?
Is email authentication set up (SPF, DKIM, DMARC)?
Are email templates responsive and accessible?
Can users manage email preferences on the web?
Is there a newsletter signup with double opt-in?
Are emails sent through a reliable provider (SendGrid, Postmark)?
Do email links work correctly (deep links to web app)?
Is there a custom 404 page that helps users find what they need?
Does the 500 error page provide useful information without leaking details?
Are client-side errors caught and reported?
Does the web app handle network disconnection gracefully?
Are form errors displayed inline (not just at top)?
Does the app retry failed API calls with backoff?
Are error pages accessible and styled consistently?
Is the login/signup flow clear and secure?
Does the web app support multiple auth methods (email, OAuth)?
Is there rate limiting on auth endpoints?
Does the app handle session expiration gracefully?
Are passwords handled securely (never logged, hashed server-side)?
Is there account recovery (password reset, email change)?
Does the web app support WebAuthn/passkeys?
Is the web copy clear, concise, and jargon-free?
Does every page have a clear purpose and call-to-action?
Is the content structured for scanability (headings, bullets, short paragraphs)?
Are features explained in terms of user benefits (not technical specs)?
Is there a blog with valuable content for the target audience?
Does the documentation balance comprehensiveness with readability?
Is the tone consistent across all web pages?
Do shared URLs generate attractive social media cards?
Are Open Graph images sized correctly for each platform?
Does the Twitter Card preview show useful information?
Can users share specific pages with pre-filled social text?
Are social sharing buttons accessible and non-intrusive?
Does LinkedIn preview show the correct title and description?
Are UTM parameters tracked for social campaign effectiveness?
Do all forms use proper HTML5 input types (email, tel, url)?
Is form validation inline and helpful (not just 'invalid input')?
Do forms work with browser autofill?
Are forms accessible (labels, fieldsets, error announcements)?
Is there progress indication for multi-step forms?
Do forms prevent double submission?
Are form data submissions secure (CSRF tokens, HTTPS)?
Does the site work without cookies (cookie-free first)?
Is localStorage used responsibly (not for sensitive data)?
Is there a cookie consent banner (if cookies are used)?
Are session tokens stored securely (HttpOnly, Secure, SameSite)?
Does the site respect DNT (Do Not Track) headers?
Is IndexedDB used for larger client-side storage?
Are storage quotas handled gracefully?
Does the site have a print stylesheet?
Are navigation, footer, and decorative elements hidden in print?
Are links shown with their URLs in print?
Is the print layout appropriate for A4/Letter paper?
Are evidence reports printable with correct formatting?
Does print preserve the semantic structure of content?
Are print styles tested across browsers?
Does the site use hreflang tags for language versions?
Are URLs localized (/es/pricing, /fr/docs)?
Does the site detect and suggest the user's language?
Are RTL layouts correct for Arabic, Hebrew, Farsi pages?
Are date/time/currency localized in the web UI?
Is there a language switcher accessible from all pages?
Does the site handle URL-based locale switching correctly?
Are custom elements used for reusable UI components?
Do web components encapsulate styles (Shadow DOM)?
Are web components accessible (shadow DOM + ARIA)?
Can web components be used by third-party app makers?
Do web components work across all supported browsers?
Are web components server-rendered for SEO?
Is there a component library/storybook for developers?
Is DNS configured correctly (A, CNAME, MX records)?
Is there a CAA record restricting certificate issuance?
Is DNSSEC enabled for the domain?
Are subdomains configured correctly (www, api, docs)?
Is there a redirect from www to apex (or vice versa)?
Are DNS TTLs appropriate for the update frequency?
Is there DNS-based load balancing or failover?

floors.persona_domain: API rate limiting and abuse prevention
questions: Are all API endpoints rate-limited?
Are rate limits documented in API responses (headers)?
Is there IP-based and user-based rate limiting?
Does the rate limiter use a sliding window algorithm?
Are rate limit responses (429) clear about retry-after?
Is there DDoS protection (Cloudflare, Cloud Armor)?
Are rate limits appropriate for different tiers (free vs paid)?
Are WebSockets used for real-time features (approvals, status)?
Does the WebSocket connection handle reconnection gracefully?
Is there fallback to polling if WebSockets are blocked?
Are WebSocket messages authenticated?
Is there heartbeat/keepalive for connection health?
Are WebSocket connections efficient (shared, not per-feature)?
Does the WebSocket work through corporate proxies?
Are critical pages server-rendered for fast initial load?
Does SSR improve SEO (search engines see full content)?
Is there streaming SSR for faster time-to-first-byte?
Does client-side hydration work without flicker?
Are dynamic meta tags rendered server-side for social sharing?
Does SSR handle errors gracefully (no blank pages)?
Is SSR cache strategy effective for performance?
Is there uptime monitoring for the web application?
Are health check endpoints available (/health, /readiness)?
Is there alerting for downtime (PagerDuty, email)?
Are response times monitored with percentile tracking?
Is there synthetic monitoring (scheduled checks from multiple regions)?
Are error rates tracked and alerted on thresholds?
Is there a public status page for users?
Does the CI/CD pipeline deploy automatically on merge to main?
Is there a staging/QA environment for testing before production?
Can deployments be rolled back quickly?
Is there zero-downtime deployment (blue/green, rolling)?
Are deployment artifacts immutable (tagged, versioned)?
Is the deployment process documented and reproducible?
Are deployment notifications sent to the team?
Is Content-Security-Policy configured correctly?
Is X-Content-Type-Options set to nosniff?
Is X-Frame-Options or frame-ancestors CSP set?
Is Referrer-Policy configured appropriately?
Is Permissions-Policy restricting unused features?
Is X-XSS-Protection set (or CSP replaces it)?
Does the security headers score A+ on securityheaders.com?
Is there a Terms of Service page?
Is there a Privacy Policy that covers data collection?
Is there a Cookie Policy (if cookies are used)?
Is there a DPA (Data Processing Agreement) for enterprise?
Are legal pages accessible from every page (footer links)?
Is there an Acceptable Use Policy?
Are legal pages kept up to date with regulatory changes?
Is the site architecture logical and shallow (3 clicks to any page)?
Is the navigation consistent across all pages?
Is there breadcrumb navigation for deep pages?
Does the sitemap.xml include all indexable pages?
Is the footer providing useful secondary navigation?
Are internal links using descriptive anchor text?
Is there a search function for finding content?
Does the web platform support webhook delivery to app makers?
Are webhooks signed (HMAC) for verification?
Is there retry logic with exponential backoff?
Are webhook payloads documented?
Can users test webhooks with a request inspection tool?
Is there a webhook delivery log for debugging?
Are webhooks delivered asynchronously (not blocking)?
Would a GraphQL API serve app makers better than REST?
Can the existing REST API coexist with GraphQL?
Would GraphQL reduce over-fetching for mobile clients?
Is there a GraphQL schema that mirrors the REST API?
Can GraphQL subscriptions replace polling for real-time data?
Is there depth/complexity limiting for GraphQL queries?
Would a GraphQL playground improve developer experience?
Is structured logging implemented (JSON format)?
Is there distributed tracing for API requests?
Are key metrics exported to a monitoring system?
Can logs be searched and filtered efficiently?
Is there log rotation and retention policy?
Are PII fields redacted from logs?
Can the system trace a user request end-to-end?
Is Firestore the right database for the workload?
Are Firestore security rules configured correctly?
Is there a data backup and recovery strategy?
Are queries optimized with appropriate indexes?
Is data pagination implemented for large collections?
Are Firestore costs monitored and optimized?
Is there a data migration strategy for schema changes?
Is there end-to-end testing for critical web flows?
Are visual regression tests catching UI changes?
Is there API contract testing for backend endpoints?
Are accessibility tests automated (axe-core in CI)?
Is there cross-browser testing in CI?
Are performance tests part of the CI pipeline?
Is test coverage tracked and improving?
Is infrastructure defined as code (Terraform, Pulumi)?
Are deployments reproducible from IaC definitions?
Is the GCP project configured with least-privilege IAM?
Are secrets managed via Secret Manager (not environment variables)?
Is there a disaster recovery plan for the infrastructure?
Are infrastructure changes reviewed before applying?
Is the infrastructure cost-optimized for the traffic level?
Can the web app serve users globally with acceptable latency?
Is there multi-region deployment for redundancy?
Are static assets served from edge locations near users?
Does the database support multi-region reads?
Is there geographic routing for API requests?
Can the system failover between regions automatically?
Are data residency requirements handled for different regions?
Can the web app handle 10x current traffic?
Is auto-scaling configured correctly?
Are there load tests simulating peak traffic?
Is the database scaled for anticipated growth?
Are there cost projections for scaling?
Can the system handle viral traffic spikes?
Is there a capacity planning document?
Can the web app be migrated to a different cloud provider?
Is there vendor lock-in with GCP services?
Can the app run on any container platform (not just Cloud Run)?
Is the data exportable from Firestore?
Can users self-host the web application?
Is the deployment portable (Docker-based)?
Are there migration guides for different hosting options?
Does the web app comply with GDPR for EU users?
Is there CCPA compliance for California users?
Does the site meet ePrivacy directive requirements?
Is there an accessible privacy dashboard for data subjects?
Are data deletion requests handled within 30 days?
Is there a Data Protection Impact Assessment?
Does the web app comply with accessibility laws (ADA, EAA)?
Could WebRTC enable real-time screen sharing with the cloud twin?
Would WebRTC reduce latency compared to WebSocket for interactive use?
Can WebRTC be used for peer-to-peer desktop ↔ mobile sync?
Is TURN/STUN server infrastructure needed?
Would WebRTC enable voice commands from the web?
Can WebRTC handle NAT traversal in enterprise networks?
Is WebRTC supported in all target browsers?
Could edge functions reduce API latency globally?
Are there functions that should run at the edge (auth, locale)?
Can edge computing reduce backend costs?
Is there a CDN with edge function support (Cloudflare Workers)?
Can edge functions handle A/B testing?
Are edge function cold start times acceptable?
Can edge functions access Firestore efficiently?
Could the evidence chain be anchored to a blockchain for immutability?
Would Web3 wallet integration be useful for authentication?
Can decentralized storage (IPFS) be used for evidence archival?
Would cryptocurrency payments be a tier option?
Can smart contracts enforce OAuth3 scope agreements?
Is there demand for Web3 integration from the target audience?
Would Web3 features be a competitive differentiator or a distraction?
Is the backend architecture appropriate for the current scale?
Would microservices improve scalability or add unnecessary complexity?
Are service boundaries aligned with business domains?
Is there service-to-service authentication?
Can services be deployed independently?
Is there an API gateway for routing and rate limiting?
Are service dependencies managed to prevent cascading failures?
Is there a dedicated developer portal for API consumers?
Can developers register for API keys through the web?
Is there a sandbox environment accessible from the web?
Does the developer portal show usage metrics and billing?
Are SDKs downloadable from the developer portal?
Is there a developer community forum on the web?
Can developers test webhooks from the portal?
What is the visitor → download conversion rate?
What is the download → active user conversion rate?
Are there clear conversion paths on every page?
Is the pricing page optimized for conversion?
Are there trust signals (security badges, testimonials)?
Is the checkout flow minimal and frictionless?
Are there win-back mechanisms for churned visitors?
Does the web presence make people want to try Solace Browser?
Is the web experience compelling enough to convert visitors?
Does the cloud twin demonstrate AI browsing without installation?
Is the web the best funnel for user acquisition?
Does the web experience match the quality of the desktop app?
Would the Dragon Rider be proud to share the website?
Is the web the universal gateway to the Solace ecosystem?
Can a non-technical visitor understand what Solace Browser does in 10 seconds?
Can they find the download button easily?
Does the website feel trustworthy (not sketchy or overly techy)?
Can they understand the pricing without a PhD in cloud computing?
Would they feel comfortable entering their email to sign up?
Does the website work well on their phone?
Would a non-technical visitor tell a friend about this website?
